name: Pauoa Flats Trail
tagline: A misty forest trail to a rare flat overlook above Nu'uanu Valley — the trailhead most people walk right past.
parking: Free roadside parking along Tantalus Drive (Round Top Drive) near the trailhead
bestTime: Early morning when the mist hangs in the valley — bring a rain layer
talkStory: About 2 miles round trip with 500 ft of moderate climbing through native forest to Pauoa Flats — a rare level clearing in the Ko'olaus with views into Nu'uanu Valley and out to the Pali. The trail connects to Aihualama and Nu'uanu Trail for longer loops through the Tantalus trail network. It's well-maintained and shaded the whole way. The upper section has some of the best native Hawaiian forest accessible from Honolulu — look for 'ohi'a lehua and koa trees. Almost always cooler and wetter than town.
